quick_facts.label: Award structure
quick_facts.value: Students enter an integrated first-degree M.Sc. programme after Class 12; an intermediate B.Sc. is not awarded. M.Sc. students may be allotted a second B.E. degree under BITS Pilani’s dual-degree scheme.

quick_facts.label: M.Sc. disciplines covered by the dual-degree scheme
quick_facts.value: For the 2026 admission framework, the scheme covers M.Sc. Biological Sciences, Chemistry, Economics, Mathematics, Physics, and Semiconductor and Nanoscience, with a second B.E. programme allotted after the first year.

quick_facts.label: Normal time to complete both degrees
quick_facts.value: The requirements for the M.Sc. and B.E. degrees are structured for normal completion in five years; BITS also notes that dual-degree students may take about 5 to 5½ years depending on academic pace and programme requirements.

quick_facts.label: 2026 academic eligibility
quick_facts.value: For M.Sc. admission through BITSAT-2026, applicants need Class 12/equivalent with Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, a language, and one additional subject; minimum 75% aggregate in PCM and at least 60% in each PCM subject are required.

quick_facts.label: 2026–27 domestic fee context
quick_facts.value: For students admitted in 2026–27 at Pilani, Goa, or Hyderabad, the listed first-degree admission fee is ₹64,100 and semester fee is ₹291,500 per semester, before hostel, mess/electricity advances, other advances, and applicable additional charges. BITS states that tuition, summer-term, and hostel fees are revised upward annually; a student admitted to the second B.E. degree also pays that programme’s admission fee when admitted.

sections.heading: Eligibility for entry through BITSAT
paragraphs: For the 2026–27 first-degree admission cycle, the relevant entry route is BITSAT. An applicant to an M.Sc. programme must have completed Class 12 under the 10+2 system from a recognised Central or State board, or an equivalent qualification, with at least five subjects including Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, one language, and one further subject. The stated academic threshold is 75% aggregate in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics (PCM), with at least 60% in each of those three subjects; theory and practical marks are both considered.

The 2026 eligibility page limits BITSAT-2026 eligibility to candidates appearing in Class 12 in 2026 or who passed in 2025, subject to its rules on first attempt, improvement examinations, result availability, and pass status. These cycle-specific rules should be rechecked for later BITSAT years.
bullets: PCM—not PCB alone—is the relevant subject combination for M.Sc. entry and for the later B.E. dual-degree pathway.
Meeting the academic eligibility minimum does not itself secure admission: allocation is merit-based through the applicable BITS admission process.

sections.heading: What “Integrated M.Sc. (BITS)” means—and when it becomes a five-year dual degree
paragraphs: BITS describes its M.Sc. programmes as integrated first degrees: they are entered after Class 12 and do not confer an intermediate B.Sc. The standard standalone integrated M.Sc. structure is designed for eight semesters, although BITS’s modular system permits variation in an individual student’s pace.

The canonical five-year route is created when an M.Sc. student receives and accepts a second B.E. degree under the dual-degree scheme. BITS states that the academic requirements for the two degrees are structured for normal completion in five years. Its broader education-process document gives an approximate range of 5 to 5½ years (10–11 semesters), so “five years” is a normal planning duration, not an unconditional guarantee.
bullets: Standalone M.Sc. integrated first degree: normally planned around eight semesters.
M.Sc. plus B.E. dual degree: normally planned around five years, potentially longer depending on registration pace and academic circumstances.

sections.heading: Curriculum and experiential learning
paragraphs: The programme combines an M.Sc. discipline with the requirements of the later-allotted B.E. discipline. BITS’s curriculum materials show composite, semester-wise patterns that retain the science first-degree coursework while adding engineering requirements; the exact course sequence depends on the selected M.Sc., the allotted B.E. branch, campus, and the student’s cohort. BITS therefore publishes programme-specific academic patterns rather than one identical syllabus for every dual-degree combination.

The broader integrated-first-degree education model includes common foundational study and electives. BITS states that students complete at least 12 electives across humanities, discipline, and open-elective categories. Integrated first-degree students can also choose the Practice School option: PS-I is two months after the second year and PS-II is five and a half months in the final year; BITS describes these as faculty-supervised, credit-bearing work at professional locations. A thesis may be an alternative to Practice School in relevant cases.
bullets: Science component: M.Sc. Biological Sciences, Chemistry, Economics, Mathematics, Physics, or Semiconductor and Nanoscience, as applicable.
Engineering component: a B.E. programme allotted through the internal dual-degree process.
Applied component: optional Practice School pathway, with PS-I and PS-II, or a permitted thesis alternative.
Course plans and degree requirements can change by cohort; students should use the academic bulletin and their registration advice as the controlling sources.

sections.heading: Admission and B.E. branch allotment
paragraphs: For domestic applicants, BITS identifies BITSAT—a computer-based online examination—as the primary merit route into integrated first-degree programmes, alongside a Board Topper scheme. Applicants submit one set of programme and campus preferences for the Pilani, K. K. Birla Goa, and Hyderabad campuses; offers are processed on merit, stated preferences, available facilities, and seats.

Admission to an M.Sc. programme does not mean that a particular B.E. branch is locked at entry. BITS states that every admitted M.Sc. student is accommodated in the dual-degree scheme for a second B.E. degree, with allocation made separately by campus after first-year performance using the Progressive Branching Index (PBI). Thus, the M.Sc. is the initial admission choice, while the engineering branch is competitively allotted after the first year and depends on first-year performance and branch-seat availability.

The 2026 BITSAT admissions cycle is time-sensitive. As of the official BITS admissions updates posted in July 2026, first-degree admission iterations were in progress; applicants should treat dates, cut-offs, fees, and iteration instructions as live information and check the official admissions portal rather than relying on prior-year schedules.
bullets: Choose and rank M.Sc. programmes/campuses in the BITS admissions preference process.
Complete the first year at the allotted campus.
Compete for the B.E. branch through campus-specific PBI-based dual-degree allocation.
Pay the additional second-programme admission fee when admitted to the B.E. degree.

sections.heading: Fees and budgeting considerations
paragraphs: For domestic students entering first-degree programmes in academic year 2026–27, BITS lists the same base charges across Pilani, Goa, and Hyderabad: ₹64,100 admission fee, ₹291,500 semester fee in each of the first two semesters, ₹36,000 hostel fee per semester for on-campus students, ₹10,000 mess and electricity advance per semester, ₹12,000 other advances per semester, ₹3,000 institute caution deposit, and annual Students’ Union and Student Aid Fund charges. Mess/electricity and other advances are described as adjustable, so they should not be treated as final consumption costs.

This is dated fee context, not a five-year fee quote. BITS expressly states that semester tuition, summer-term, and hostel fees for the 2026–27 entrant cohort will rise each year. A dual-degree student also pays the admission fee for the second B.E. programme at the time it is allotted. Costs can further vary with summer-term registration, Practice School, campus living arrangements, taxes or new levies, and personal expenses.
bullets: Use the official 2026–27 fee schedule for current components and payment timing.
Budget for annual fee revisions rather than multiplying first-year charges by five.
Include the second-programme admission fee if a B.E. dual-degree allotment is accepted.

sections.heading: Further study, examinations, and career directions
paragraphs: The combined science-and-engineering curriculum can support several directions represented by this node’s children: postgraduate engineering or science study (including an M.S. abroad or Ph.D.), software and data roles, graduate-engineer and technical consulting roles, research-organisation roles, entrepreneurship, and management or public-service examinations. These are pathways to investigate rather than automatic outcomes; selection depends on academic record, skills, projects, experience, entrance tests, recruitment criteria, and the requirements of each destination institution or employer.

For research progression, BITS Pilani’s 2026–27 Ph.D. brochure lists M.Sc., B.E., and equivalent qualifications with at least 60% aggregate among the minimum entry qualifications, while noting that departments may apply additional shortlisting criteria and that minimum eligibility does not guarantee admission. For government-research careers, current CSIR Scientist Recruitment and Assessment Promotion Rules show that Scientist-B requirements vary by stream: science posts may require an M.Sc. or equivalent, engineering posts a B.E./B.Tech. or equivalent, and some streams have distinct requirements. This illustrates why graduates should read the recruitment notice for the exact discipline and post.

For examination-led transitions, graduates should verify the relevant annual notification. UPSC ran the Civil Services (Preliminary) Examination 2026 under its published schedule; CAT and management-school requirements, as well as civil-services rules, are set by their respective current notifications and institutes. The degree itself does not replace exam-specific eligibility, performance, or selection requirements.
bullets: MS in Engineering abroad or Ph.D. in Science: build research exposure, strong grades, recommendations, and meet the destination university’s stated admissions requirements.

Software Developer, Data Scientist, Graduate Engineer Trainee, Management Consultant, or Scientist roles: use the M.Sc.–B.E. combination alongside internships/Practice School, projects, technical preparation, and each employer’s recruitment criteria.

Tech Startup Founder: pursue through independent venture-building; BITS identifies entrepreneurship and innovation support as part of its student ecosystem, but startup outcomes are inherently individual and uncertain.
CAT and UPSC Civil Services Examination: track the official yearly eligibility rules, registration dates, syllabus, and selection process.
